questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'this' keyword in Java?
options: A) To access local variables
B) To refer to the current object
C) To access static variables
D) To declare new variables
questions.explanation: The 'this' keyword is used to refer to the current object in a class, allowing methods to access its properties and fields.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of encapsulation in Java?
options: A) Accessing a field directly
B) Hiding a field with a getter and setter method
C) Extending a class
D) Implementing an interface
questions.explanation: Encapsulation is demonstrated when a field is hidden from direct access and instead accessed through a getter and setter method.

questions.question: What does the term 'inheritance' refer to in object-oriented programming?
options: A) The creation of a new class
B) The extension of an existing class
C) The implementation of an interface
D) The deletion of a class
questions.explanation: Inheritance is a mechanism where a new class can inherit the properties and behavior of an existing class, also known as the parent class.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of polymorphism in Java?
options: A) A method that performs different actions based on the type of input
B) A method that throws an exception if the input is null
C) A method that takes a variable number of arguments
D) A method that returns a fixed value
questions.explanation: Polymorphism is demonstrated when a method can take on multiple forms based on the type of input or context in which it is used.

questions.question: What is the term for hiding implementation details in an object-oriented program?
options: A) Abstraction
B) Encapsulation
C) Inheritance
D) Polymorphism
questions.explanation: Abstraction is a concept in object-oriented programming where implementation details are hidden from the user, exposing only the necessary information.
